senses.definition: surgical removal of a limb or a part of it
a surgical procedure in which a surgeon creates a new pathway for the transport of substances in the body
the surgical removal of the protecting loose skin of a male's penis
a medical procedure that involves widening narrowed or blocked blood vessels, typically performed on arteries, to improve blood flow

examples.x: The nurse explained that angioplasty is like a highway cleanup for blood vessels.
Grandma's angioplasty helped prevent a heart attack by clearing her blocked arteries.

senses.definition: a surgical procedure in which the appendix gets removed

examples.x: Tim needed an appendectomy when his appendix became swollen and painful.
The surgeon performed an appendectomy to prevent further complications.
Sarah's appendectomy was a success, and she was grateful for the relief it brought.
The doctor recommended an appendectomy when Mark's stomach pain wouldn't go away.
In emergency cases, an appendectomy is often the recommended course of action for severe abdominal pain.

senses.definition: a surgical procedure where an opening is made in the abdomen to redirect part of the colon for waste elimination

examples.x: After the surgery, Dad had a colostomy to help with his digestion.
Lacey adjusted well to life with a colostomy after recovering from surgery.
Amy's doctor suggested a colostomy to manage her bowel issues.
The doctor recommended a colostomy to improve Mike's digestive health.
Learning about colostomy care helped Sarah support her friend during recovery.

senses.definition: a surgical procedure to remove tissue from the inner lining of the uterus

examples.x: The doctor recommended a dilation and curettage to address the abnormal bleeding.
After the miscarriage, she underwent a dilation and curettage procedure.
Dilation and curettage is sometimes performed to diagnose uterine conditions.
The gynecologist explained the risks and benefits of dilation and curettage to the patient.
The procedure, dilation and curettage, is typically done under anesthesia in a hospital setting.

senses.definition: a medical procedure to align a person's physical characteristics with their gender identity, often used by transgender individuals
a procedure that creates new pathways for blood flow around blocked or narrowed coronary arteries to improve heart muscle blood supply
a surgical procedure in which a woman's uterus is removed, often performed for medical reasons such as to treat certain health conditions

examples.x: Recovery after a hysterectomy can vary, but many women resume normal activities.
Sarah's hysterectomy improved her quality of life by eliminating discomfort.
After years of health issues, Mary decided to have a hysterectomy.
The doctor recommended a hysterectomy to treat Lisa's chronic pain.
Cindy had a hysterectomy to address a medical issue affecting her uterus.

senses.definition: a minimally invasive surgical procedure that uses a small camera to view and treat conditions inside the abdomen or pelvis

examples.x: Julie's infertility was addressed through laparoscopy to treat underlying issues.
After the laparoscopy, Mike was back to work sooner than he expected.
During laparoscopy, a small camera helped the doctor see inside Jane's belly.
The benefits of laparoscopy include less pain and a shorter hospital stay.

senses.definition: a surgical procedure that involves making a larger cut in the abdomen to access and treat internal organs or address certain medical conditions

examples.x: After the accident, John needed a laparotomy to repair internal injuries.
Her surgeon performed a laparotomy to remove a tumor from her abdomen.
Susan had a laparotomy to address complications during childbirth.
Recovery after laparotomy may take longer due to the larger incision.
The surgeon discussed the risks and benefits of laparotomy before the procedure.

senses.definition: a type of weight-loss surgery that that aims to help manage obesity by shrinking the size of someone's stomach so that they will eat less food
a surgical procedure by which a doctor replaces a missing or damaged tissue or bone with a healthy substitute
a surgical operation that is performed in order to treat severe mental disorders by cutting into part of a person's brain
a surgical procedure in which a small, localized mass or tumor, typically in the breast, is removed while preserving the surrounding tissue

examples.x: Emily had a lumpectomy to remove a small lump from her breast.
After the lumpectomy, Anne's doctor confirmed the absence of cancerous cells.
The lumpectomy allowed Lucy to preserve most of her breast tissue.
During the lumpectomy, the doctor aimed to remove only the abnormal tissue.
The surgeon explained the benefits and potential risks of lumpectomy before the procedure.

senses.definition: a precise surgical technique that uses a microscope for intricate procedures on small structures

examples.x: Mark's delicate eye surgery involved microsurgery to fix the retina.
Tom's hand injury required microsurgery to repair the delicate nerves.
The surgeon used microsurgery to reattach blood vessels during the procedure.
During microsurgery, the doctor repaired a small tear in the patient's artery.

senses.definition: any type of surgery that requires making a large incision in the chest wall to make the heart easier for the surgeon to reach
a device or material inserted into the body, often for medical or technological purposes, such as a computer chip

examples.x: The implant allowed the patient to regain their hearing.
Scientists are testing computer chip implants in humans.
Some people are skeptical about using implants for identification.
Computer chip implants are becoming more common in healthcare.

senses.definition: a surgical cut made in flesh or skin
a surgical procedure to reconstruct or create a vagina, commonly used for gender affirmation or medical purposes
a surgical procedure to remove stones from the urinary tract

examples.x: Mark's lithotomy allowed the surgeon to address his bladder issues.
Sarah underwent lithotomy for a gynecological procedure.

senses.definition: a surgical procedure that involves removing part of the vertebral bone, called the lamina, to relieve pressure on the spinal cord or nerves

examples.x: After his injury, James had a laminectomy to ease pressure on his spinal nerves.
The doctor recommended laminectomy to treat the bulging disc causing numbness.
Laminectomy provided relief for David's sciatic pain by addressing spinal issues.
During laminectomy, the surgeon carefully removed the bone to free the compressed nerves.
After laminectomy, Emily's physical therapy focused on strengthening her back.

senses.definition: a surgical procedure that removes part of a vertebral lamina to relieve spinal nerve pressure or treat spinal conditions
a surgical operation performed on the nervous system, particularly the brain and spinal cord
a surgical procedure that involves the removal of the tonsils, often performed to alleviate recurrent throat infections or breathing difficulties

examples.x: Lucy's tonsillectomy improved her overall throat health.
The surgeon performed a tonsillectomy to address chronic tonsillitis.
Sarah had a tonsillectomy to stop frequent sore throats.
Emily's doctor recommended a tonsillectomy for persistent infections.
Mary's tonsillectomy reduced the number of times she got sick.

senses.definition: a surgical procedure that involves creating an opening in the neck to provide a direct airway, often done to assist with breathing in cases of airway obstruction or respiratory problems

examples.x: After a severe accident, John had a tracheotomy to help him breathe.
During the tracheotomy, a small tube was inserted to aid Tom's airflow.
Mark's tracheotomy allowed him to receive oxygen directly through the neck.
Lucy's doctor performed a tracheotomy to assist her in overcoming breathing difficulties.
Emily's doctor recommended a tracheotomy for her prolonged respiratory issues.

senses.definition: a surgical operation for male sterilization or permanent contraception
a medical procedure involving the transplantation of organs from animals, usually pigs, to humans to address organ shortages

examples.x: Emily's successful xenotransplant allowed her to breathe without difficulty.
Bob underwent a xenotransplant to receive a pig heart.
After the xenotransplant, Sarah's kidney function improved.

senses.definition: a surgical procedure to replace defective or damaged organs by transplanting or implanting artificial ones
a minimally invasive procedure that uses an endoscope to treat sinus-related conditions by removing obstructions or enlarging sinus passages
a medical procedure involving the movement of organs, tissues, or cells from one person to another to replace or repair damaged or failing body parts

examples.x: Emily received a heart transplantation to treat her cardiac condition.
After the transplantation, Mark's new kidney improved his overall health.
Mike underwent a liver transplantation to address his liver failure.
Sarah's successful lung transplantation allowed her to breathe more easily.
The surgeon performed a corneal transplantation to restore vision in Jane's eye.

senses.definition: a medical treatment that involves surgical procedures for conditions related to the jaw, face, and mouth
a procedure to remove or destroy tissue, often used for treating tumors or cardiac rhythm issues

examples.x: Linda had an ablation to eliminate an irregular heartbeat.
After the ablation, Emily's problematic tissue was successfully removed.
Tim's successful ablation resolved his kidney cyst issue.
Jane underwent ablation to correct her persistent heart rhythm issues.
Mark's doctor recommended ablation to address his lung tumor.

senses.definition: a surgical procedure involving the removal of the breast tissue, often performed as a treatment for breast cancer or as a preventive measure

examples.x: Jane's successful mastectomy addressed the early-stage breast cancer.
Tom's wife chose a mastectomy as part of her breast cancer treatment plan.
The decision to have a mastectomy is often based on individual health considerations.
The doctor explained the benefits and potential side effects of mastectomy.
Mark's doctor recommended a mastectomy to prevent future health risks.

senses.definition: a non-invasive procedure that uses high-energy radiation to precisely treat tumors or lesions without surgery
a medical procedure that uses shock waves or lasers to break down kidney or urinary tract stones without surgery
a specialized surgical technique that uses three-dimensional coordinates or imaging guidance to precisely target and treat specific areas of the body
a strong blow to the chest over the heart used to restore normal heart rhythm during cardiac arrest or arrhythmias
a procedure that clears blockages in the Eustachian tube by using positive pressure to equalize ear pressure
blood filtering for managing kidney dysfunction or acute renal failure
a medical procedure that provides temporary mechanical support to the heart and lungs by circulating and oxygenating blood outside of the body
a medical procedure that involves placing a tube into the trachea, or windpipe, to establish an open airway for breathing, often used in cases of respiratory distress or during anesthesia for surgery
a medical procedure that involves delivering fluids, medications, or nutrients directly into a person's bloodstream through a vein using a needle or catheter
a medical procedure or method that makes a person or animal incapable of reproduction

examples.x: Mike and Lisa decided on sterilization as they didn't want more children.
Sarah's cat's behavior improved after undergoing sterilization.
Jane's decision for sterilization provided a reliable method of birth control.
Mark chose sterilization to prevent unintended pregnancies.
The veterinarian discussed the benefits and aftercare of sterilization for the pet owners.

senses.definition: a surgical procedure to create a breathing passage in the neck

examples.x: The patient underwent a tracheostomy for breathing assistance.
Doctors perform tracheostomies to clear airway obstructions.
Tracheostomies allow for easier secretion suctioning.

senses.definition: a procedure to replace damaged or diseased bone marrow with healthy stem cells

examples.x: Bone marrow transplantation is often a critical treatment for patients with leukemia, providing them with healthy stem cells to replace damaged or diseased bone marrow.
After undergoing chemotherapy, she received a bone marrow transplantation to restore her body’s ability to produce healthy blood cells.
The success of bone marrow transplantation depends on finding a compatible donor, which can sometimes be a challenging and lengthy process.
Post bone marrow transplantation, the patient remained in isolation to prevent infections while their immune system gradually rebuilt itself.
Bone marrow transplantation has significantly improved the survival rates of patients with certain types of blood cancers and genetic disorders.

senses.definition: a surgical procedure to implant electrodes in the brain to treat movement disorders like Parkinson's disease

examples.x: Deep brain stimulation has shown promising results in reducing the symptoms of Parkinson's disease, allowing patients to regain better control over their movements.
The neurosurgeon explained that deep brain stimulation involves implanting electrodes in specific areas of the brain to modulate abnormal activity.
For patients with severe, treatment-resistant depression, deep brain stimulation may offer an alternative when other therapies have failed.
The effectiveness of deep brain stimulation can vary, and adjustments to the device's settings are often needed to achieve the best results.
During the deep brain stimulation procedure, the patient remained awake so the medical team could accurately target the regions of the brain causing the symptoms.
